name: sanity
description: Sanity CLI tool for managing Sanity installations, managing plugins, schemas and datasets

subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: Displays help information about Sanity

subcommands.name: build
subcommands.description: Builds the current Sanity configuration to a static bundle

options.description: Enable source maps for built bundles (increases size of bundle)
Skip minifying built JavaScript (speeds up build, increases size of bundle)
Use unattended mode, accepting defaults and using only flags for choices

subcommands.name: codemod
subcommands.description: Runs a code modification script

options.description: Dry run (no changes are made to files)
Transform files with these file extensions (comma separated list) (default: js,ts,tsx)
Skip verification before running codemod

subcommands.name: configcheck
subcommands.description: Checks if the required configuration files for plugins exists and are up to date

subcommands.name: cors
subcommands.description: Interact with CORS-entries for your project

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Allow a new origin to use your project API through CORS

options.description: Allow credentials (token/cookie) to be sent from this origin
Disallow credentials (token/cookie) to be sent from this origin

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Delete an existing CORS-origin from your project

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List all origins allowed to access the API for this project

subcommands.name: dataset
subcommands.description: Interact with datasets in your project

subcommands.name: alias
subcommands.description: You can manage your dataset alias using this command

options.description: Skips security prompt and forces link command

subcommands.name: copy
subcommands.description: Manages dataset copying, including starting a new copy job, listing copy jobs and following the progress of a running copy job

options.description: Start the copy without waiting for it to finish
Attach to the running copy process to show progress
Don't preserve document history on copy
Lists all dataset copy jobs corresponding to a certain criteria
Start position in the list of jobs. Default 0. With --list
Maximum number of jobs returned. Default 10. Maximum 1000. With --list

subcommands.name: create
subcommands.description: Create a new dataset within your project

options.description: Set visibility for this dataset (public/private)

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Delete a dataset within your project

subcommands.name: export
subcommands.description: Export dataset to local filesystem as a gzipped tarball

options.description: Extract only documents, without rewriting asset references
Export only non-asset documents and remove references to image assets
Export only published versions of documents
Skips compressing tarball entries (still generates a gzip file)
Defines which document types to export
Overwrite any file with the same name
Concurrent number of asset downloads

subcommands.name: import
subcommands.description: Import documents to given dataset from ndjson file

options.description: On duplicate document IDs, skip importing document in question
On duplicate document IDs, replace existing document with imported document
Skip assets that cannot be fetched/uploaded
Skip reuse of existing assets

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List datasets of your project

subcommands.name: visibility
subcommands.description: Set visibility of a dataset

subcommands.name: debug
subcommands.description: Gathers information on Sanity environment

options.description: Include API keys in output

subcommands.name: deploy
subcommands.description: Deploys a statically built Sanity studio

options.description: Enable source maps for built bundles (increases size of bundle)
Skip minifying built JavaScript (speeds up build, increases size of bundle)
Don't build the studio prior to deploy, instead deploying the version currently in `dist/`

subcommands.name: docs
subcommands.description: Opens the Sanity documentation

subcommands.name: documents
subcommands.description: Interact with documents in your project

subcommands.name: create
subcommands.description: Create one or more documents

options.description: On duplicate document IDs, replace existing document with specified document(s)
On duplicate document IDs, don't modify the target document(s)
Write the documents whenever the target file or buffer changes
Use JSON5 file type to allow a "simplified" version of JSON
Specify a document ID to use. Will fetch remote document ID and populate editor
NAME to override dataset

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Delete a document by ID

options.description: NAME to override dataset

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: Get and print a document by ID

options.description: Colorized JSON output
NAME to override dataset

subcommands.name: query
subcommands.description: Query for documents

options.description: Colorized JSON output
NAME to override dataset
API version to use (defaults to `v1`)

subcommands.name: exec
subcommands.description: Runs a script in Sanity context

options.description: Preload access token from CLI config into 'part:@sanity/base/client' part
Mocks a browser-like environment using jsdom

subcommands.name: graphql
subcommands.description: Interact with GraphQL APIs

subcommands.name: deploy
subcommands.description: Deploy a GraphQL API from the current Sanity schema

options.description: Deploy API for the given dataset
Deploy API to given tag (defaults to 'default')
API generation to deploy (defaults to 'gen3')
Set document interface fields (_id, _type etc) as non-null
Deploy a GraphQL playground for easily testing queries (public)
Skip playground prompt (do not deploy a playground)
Deploy API without confirming breaking changes

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: Lists all the GraphQL endpoints deployed for this project

subcommands.name: undeploy
subcommands.description: Remove a deployed GraphQL API

options.description: Delete GraphQL API for the given dataset
Delete GraphQL API for the given tag (defaults to 'default')

subcommands.name: hook
subcommands.description: Interact with hooks in your project

subcommands.name: attempt
subcommands.description: Print details of a given webhook delivery attempt

subcommands.name: create
subcommands.description: Create a new hook for the given dataset

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Delete a hook within your project

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List hooks for a given project

subcommands.name: logs
subcommands.description: List latest log entries for a given hook

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Initialize a new Sanity project or plugin

options.description: Use unattended mode, accepting defaults and using only flags for choices
Project ID to use for the studio
Organization ID to use for the project
Dataset name for the studio
Set up a project with a public dataset named "production"
Path to write studio project to
Project template to use [default: "clean"]
Login provider to use
Visibility mode for dataset (public/private)
Create a new project with the given name
Optionally select a plan for a new project
Optionally select a coupon for a new project (cannot be used with --project-plan)
Reconfigure Sanity studio in current folder with new project/dataset

subcommands.name: install
subcommands.description: Installs a Sanity plugin to the current Sanity configuration

subcommands.name: login
subcommands.description: Authenticates against the Sanity.io API (no flag) or a third-party identity provider (with --sso flag)

options.description: Authenticate against a third-party identity provider

subcommands.name: logout
subcommands.description: Logs out of the Sanity.io session

subcommands.name: manage
subcommands.description: Opens the Sanity project management UI

subcommands.name: projects
subcommands.description: Interact with projects connected to your logged in user

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: Lists projects connected to your user

options.description: Sort output by specified column
Sort output ascending/descending

subcommands.name: start
subcommands.description: Starts a web server for the Content Studio

options.description: TCP port to start server on. [default: 3333]
The local network interface at which to listen. [default: "127.0.0.1"]

subcommands.name: undeploy
subcommands.description: Removes the deployed studio from <hostname>.sanity.studio

subcommands.name: uninstall
subcommands.description: Removes a Sanity plugin from the current Sanity configuration

subcommands.name: upgrade
subcommands.description: Upgrades all (or some) Sanity modules to their latest versions

options.description: Version range to upgrade to, eg '^2.2.7' or '2.1.x'
Tagged release to upgrade to, eg 'canary' or 'some-feature'
Pin the resolved version numbers in package.json (no ^ prefix)

subcommands.name: users
subcommands.description: Manage users of your project

subcommands.name: invite
subcommands.description: Invite a new user to the project

options.description: Role to invite the user as

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List users of the project

options.description: Don't include pending invitations
Don't include robots (token users)
Sort users by specified column: id, name, role, date
Sort output ascending/descending

subcommands.name: versions
subcommands.description: Shows the installed versions of Sanity CLI and core components

options.description: Show help for sanity
